skill.name: python-executor
skill.description: Execute Python code in a safe sandboxed environment via inference.sh with 100+ pre-installed libraries for data processing, web scraping, image manipulation, and automation.
skill.summary: Run Python code remotely in a sandboxed environment with extensive library support.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 69 patterns in SKILL.md documentation file, but all are false positives. The 'backtick execution' patterns are markdown code block formatting, not shell execution. Network patterns are documentation URLs and example snippets. The skill legitimately uses the infsh CLI to send Python code to a remote sandboxed execution service (inference.sh). Risk is medium due to remote code execution capability, but execution occurs in an isolated subprocess on their infrastructure with timeout limits (1-300s).

medium_findings.title: Remote Code Execution Service
medium_findings.description: This skill enables arbitrary Python code execution via a remote service (inference.sh). While the execution is sandboxed, users should be aware that code runs on external infrastructure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly uses 'allowed-tools: Bash(infsh *)' to execute code remotely via the infsh CLI - this is the core functionality.

content.user_title: Execute Python code in a sandboxed environment
content.value_statement: Running Python code locally requires setting up environments and managing dependencies. This skill provides instant access to a pre-configured Python environment with 100+ libraries ready to use.
actual_capabilities: Execute arbitrary Python code in a remote sandboxed environment
Access 100+ pre-installed libraries including NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, and OpenCV
Perform web scraping with requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, and Playwright
Process images, videos, and 3D models with specialized libraries
Generate charts, PDFs, and other output files automatically
Run automation scripts with configurable timeout (1-300 seconds)
limitations: CPU-only environment - no GPU acceleration for ML libraries
Non-interactive execution - cannot use plt.show() or interactive prompts
Maximum timeout of 300 seconds per execution
Output files must be saved to outputs/ directory to be returned

use_cases.title: Data Analysis Pipeline
use_cases.description: Process and visualize datasets using Pandas and Matplotlib without local environment setup.

use_cases.title: Automated Web Scraping
use_cases.description: Extract data from websites using requests, BeautifulSoup, or browser automation tools.

use_cases.title: Media Processing
use_cases.description: Create and manipulate images, videos, and 3D models using specialized Python libraries.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Python Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Run this Python code: print('Hello World')

prompt_templates.title: Data Analysis with Pandas
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DataFrame with columns 'name' and 'sales', then calculate the average sales and display a bar chart.

prompt_templates.title: Web Scraping Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape the title and all links from https://example.com using requests and BeautifulSoup, then return the results as JSON.

prompt_templates.title: Image Processing Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Load an image, apply a grayscale filter, resize to 800x600, and save the result. Then create a thumbnail version at 150x150 pixels.

output_examples.input: Execute: import pandas as pd; df = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1, 2], 'B': [3, 4]}); print(df.mean())
output_examples.output:
Code executed successfully. Output: A    1.5
B    3.5
dtype: float64

output_examples.input: Create a bar chart from data and save to outputs/chart.png
output_examples.output:
Code executed successfully. Output: Chart saved!
Attached files: chart.png (PNG image, 800x600)

best_practices: Always save output files to the outputs/ directory to ensure they are returned in the response
Set appropriate timeout values for long-running operations like video processing or web scraping
Use non-interactive methods like plt.savefig() instead of plt.show() for visualizations
anti_patterns: Do not attempt interactive operations like plt.show() or input() - the environment is non-interactive
Avoid relying on GPU libraries - the environment is CPU-only
Do not expect persistent state between separate code executions - each run is isolated

faq.question: What libraries are available?
faq.answer: Over 100 libraries are pre-installed including NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, Playwright, Pillow, OpenCV, MoviePy, trimesh, and many more for data science, web scraping, and media processing.

faq.question: How do I get output files?
faq.answer: Save any files you want to retrieve to the outputs/ directory. Files saved there are automatically detected and returned with the execution results.

faq.question: Can I install additional packages?
faq.answer: No, only the pre-installed libraries are available. If you need a specific package, check the documentation or contact the skill author.

faq.question: What is the maximum execution time?
faq.answer: The timeout can be set between 1 and 300 seconds. The default is 30 seconds. For longer operations like video processing, use the timeout parameter.

faq.question: Is my code secure?
faq.answer: Code runs in an isolated subprocess on inference.sh infrastructure. However, avoid sending sensitive data like API keys or credentials in your code.

faq.question: Can I use this for machine learning?
faq.answer: This environment is CPU-only and not suitable for training ML models. Use dedicated AI skills for machine learning tasks. Basic inference with CPU-compatible libraries may work for small models.
